perturbation.original_text: Schneider hereby further represents and warrants to the Company that: (a) he satisfies all of the qualifications to be a director of the Company as set forth in Article III, Section 4 of the Company's bylaws and any additional applicable qualifications under the laws of the State of Washington or under the regulations of any bank regulatory authority, and that he is not in any way precluded from serving as a director by order or other action of any court, -1- regulatory or other governmental authority; and (b) no event has occurred with respect to Schneider that would require disclosure in a document filed by the Company with the SEC pursuant to the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, under Item 401(f) of SEC Regulation S-K.
perturbation.changed_text: Schneider hereby further represents and warrants to the Company that: (a) he satisfies some of the qualifications to be a director of the Company as set forth in Article III, Section 4 of the Company's bylaws; and (b) no event has occurred with respect to Schneider that would require disclosure in a document filed by the Company with the SEC.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'all of the qualifications' to 'some of the qualifications' and removing the clause concerning Washington state law, regulations and Item 401(f) of SEC Regulation S-K, there's uncertainty about Schneider's full qualifications as a director and potential SEC disclosure requirements. This could contradict a requirement elsewhere (implied or stated) for directors to fully meet all legal and regulatory standards. It also leads to internal contradiction, since it is not specified what 'some of the qualifications' entail or satisfy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schneider hereby further represents and warrants to the Company that: (a) he satisfies some of the qualifications to be a director of the Company as set forth in Article III, Section 4 of the Company's bylaws; and (b) no event has occurred with respect to Schneider that would require disclosure in a document filed by the Company with the SEC.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text from requiring 'all of the qualifications' to 'some of the qualifications' for being a director, and removes specific legal and regulatory requirements. This directly contradicts the implied or stated requirement that directors must meet all legal and regulatory standards, as highlighted in the explanation. The contradiction is strong because it creates uncertainty about the director's qualifications and potential SEC disclosure obligations, violating the expectation of complete compliance and potentially creating legal issues.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto pertaining to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations and discussions of the parties in connection therewith not referred to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes an agreement between the parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes 'entire agreement' language along with 'supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements'. This omission implies that other unwritten agreements could also be in effect or that previous agreements are still relevant, creating ambiguity. Parties can argue what agreements should be in place, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes an agreement between the parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the agreement is the 'entire agreement' and 'supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements'. The changed text removes this, implying that other agreements outside the written contract could exist and be valid. Since the contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, the omission of 'entire agreement' language creates ambiguity and directly contradicts the implied exclusivity of the agreement, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.
